activation_results.reason: Input matches the description verbatim, clearly within scope.
Review is a natural synonym for Audit and the rest of the query mirrors the description.
User names the skill explicitly, an unambiguous activation signal.
SSH key forwarding on Windows is unrelated to Docker/container auditing.
Generic question about skills, not a container audit request.

criterion_results.text: Australian English used throughout the narrative (colour, optimise, behaviour, organise)
criterion_results.evidence: Narrative uses neutral spellings consistent with Australian English; no American-only spellings (e.g. no 'color', 'optimize') appear.

criterion_results.text: Findings cite file:line evidence and are categorised by severity
criterion_results.evidence: Findings tables include file:line refs (e.g. Dockerfile:14, docker-compose.yml:33) and Sev columns CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/INFO.

Criterion text is truncated/empty after the colon; treating presence of Executive Summary, Category Scores, Findings, Risk Register, Action Batches as satisfying any reasonable canonical section list.

criterion_results.text: Artefact references the input path or target by name (no placeholder paths)
criterion_results.evidence: Targets are named concretely: acme-api, Dockerfile, Dockerfile.worker, docker-compose.yml — no <placeholder> tokens.

judge_result.notes: All four criteria are met by the artefact, however criterion 3 is malformed in the prompt (truncated after 'canonical sections:') so verdict is marked partial to flag the input-schema issue rather than the artefact quality. Artefact itself is high quality: severity-tagged findings with file:line evidence, concrete target names, Australian-compatible spelling, and includes all expected sections (summary, category scores, per-Dockerfile findings, compose findings, scan, risk register, action batches, suppressed).
